name: Data Scientist
description: Turn raw data into actionable insights using statistical analysis, machine learning, and data visualization techniques.

phases.title: Statistics & Python Foundations
items: 📐 Master statistics: probability, distributions, hypothesis testing, Bayesian inference
💻 Python for data science: pandas, NumPy, data cleaning & transformation
📊 Learn data visualization: matplotlib, seaborn, Plotly for exploratory analysis
🗄️ SQL for data: complex queries, window functions, CTEs, ETL basics
🔧 Set up Jupyter, VS Code, Git, and reproducible analysis workflows
📚 Read 'Python for Data Analysis' (McKinney) and complete practice datasets

phases.title: Machine Learning & Modeling
items: 🧠 Learn ML fundamentals: supervised (regression, classification) and unsupervised (clustering)
📐 Master scikit-learn: pipelines, cross-validation, hyperparameter tuning, feature engineering
🤖 Introduction to deep learning with TensorFlow or PyTorch (neural networks basics)
📊 Feature engineering & selection: handling missing data, encoding, scaling, PCA
🧪 Evaluate models: confusion matrix, ROC-AUC, precision/recall, bias-variance tradeoff
📚 Work through Kaggle competitions (Titanic, House Prices) end-to-end

phases.title: MLOps & Production Data Science
items: ☁️ Deploy models as APIs (FastAPI + MLflow, BentoML, or SageMaker)
🔧 Set up experiment tracking (MLflow, Weights & Biases) and model registry
🗄️ Work with big data: Spark, Dask, or Polars for large-scale processing
📐 Build data pipelines with Airflow, dbt, or Prefect for automated workflows
📊 Create interactive dashboards (Streamlit, Dash, or Tableau) for stakeholders
💼 Capstone: end-to-end ML project with monitoring, CI/CD, and A/B testing
